contract.name: v2/disaster/overview.md
contract.description: Aggregates disaster-related budget authority and spending totals for selected Disaster Emergency Fund (DEFC) codes and may expose unlabeled rollups under 'additional'.

def_codes.description: Comma-separated list of DEFC codes to aggregate. Accepts numeric and uppercase multi-character tokens; invalid or case-mismatched codes are ignored without error.
def_codes.constraints: At least one token is required in practice. Blank strings return 422 and omitting the parameter runs until a 504 timeout. Leading whitespace on the overall parameter is trimmed, but per-token spaces prevent matches. Repeating the key keeps only the final string. Array-style keys (def_codes[]) trigger 504 gateway errors. Large selections or heavy codes such as 'O' or 'Q' frequently time out once before succeeding on retry.

funding.description: Aggregated funding totals per DEFC. Entries appear only when the amount is non-zero, amounts may be negative, and ordering does not follow request order or amount magnitude.

total_budget_authority.description: Sum of budget authority attributed to the matched DEFC codes; equals the sum of reported funding amounts and can be negative.

spending.description: Totals for obligations and outlays. All fields are always present and numeric but may be zero or internally inconsistent.

additional.description: Optional rollup for amounts lacking the requested DEFC labels. When present it contains its own budget authority and spending totals.

body.detail: Field 'def_codes' value '' is below min '1' items

quirks: Requests without def_codes or targeting heavy codes (e.g., 'O', 'Q') often run for about a minute before returning a 504 HTML timeout; retrying immediately can succeed once the response is cached.
Whitespace handling is asymmetric: leading spaces on the overall parameter are trimmed, but spaces within the comma-separated list prevent tokens from matching, so 'L, M' only yields 'L'.
Submitting def_codes[] style array syntax results in 504 gateway errors instead of validation feedback.
funding entries may be absent for zero totals, can contain negative amounts, and appear in an order unrelated to input order or amount size.
The 'additional' field alternates between an object and null depending on which DEFC combinations are requested.
risks: Gateway timeouts are common for uncached or broad requests, so clients should implement retries with backoff and tolerate HTML error payloads.
Case or whitespace mistakes silently drop DEFC tokens, yielding zeroed aggregates without explicit errors.
Downstream logic must handle 'additional' switching between null and an object with totals.
gaps: Ordering guarantees for the funding array are undocumented and remain unexplained by the observed data.
Maximum safe breadth of the def_codes list is unknown; large selections still occasionally produce 504 responses despite caching.

probes.notes: pass – code 'V' returns populated funding and an 'additional' object containing unlabeled totals.
pass – demonstrates multi-character codes and non-deterministic funding order.
pass – blank def_codes triggers validation error proving a practical minimum of one token.
fail – omitting def_codes hangs until the gateway returns a 504 HTML timeout.
pass – internal space after comma causes the 'M' token to be ignored.
fail – first attempt for heavy code 'O' ended in a 504 before a cached retry succeeded.

mismatches: Docs describe def_codes as an optional array parameter, but the live service requires a single comma-separated string and treats it as mandatory in practice (omitted requests time out).
gaps: Ordering guarantees for the funding array are undocumented and remain unexplained by the observed data.
Maximum safe breadth of the def_codes list is unknown; large selections still occasionally produce 504 responses despite caching.
risks: Gateway timeouts are common for uncached or broad requests, so clients should implement retries with backoff and tolerate HTML error payloads.
Case or whitespace mistakes silently drop DEFC tokens, yielding zeroed aggregates without explicit errors.
Downstream logic must handle 'additional' switching between null and an object with totals.
